metadata.full_system_note: This file contains 82 fully structured contacts. An additional 76 contacts are in DISTRIBUTION_OUTREACH_CONTACTS.md (87 contacts across 5 pillars) and policy-influencer-mapping.md. Combined total: 158+ contacts across all distribution files.
metadata.description: Phase 1 distribution contact database — 35-domain Democratic Renewal Framework. Structured for immediate outreach upon user path decision. All roles, titles, and affiliations verified against public sources as of April 2026.

tiers.1: High-leverage decision-makers — senators, think tank leadership, law school clinic directors, top civil society executives. Decision authority: policy or institutional action.
tiers.2: Amplifiers — media, academics, law review editors, legal commentators. Decision authority: citation, publication, or coverage.
tiers.3: Practitioners — state AG offices, law clinics, advocacy coalitions, labor networks. Decision authority: operational adoption and implementation.

metadata.receptivity_basis: Estimated from: organizational alignment with proposal domains, public statements on democracy reform, active litigation or legislative work in relevant areas, existing citation relationships.

fields.name: Full name
fields.contact: Best available contact (email where publicly confirmed, otherwise routing note)
fields.primary_domains: Most relevant proposal domains for this contact
fields.batch: Phase 1 outreach batch (1=first week, 2=second week, 3=third week, 4=Tier 2 wave)
fields.notes: Personalization hooks, relationship to framework, warm intro paths

contacts.name: Ryan Goodman
contacts.organization: Just Security / NYU School of Law
contacts.role: Co-Editor-in-Chief (Just Security); Anne and Joel Ehrenkranz Professor of Law
contacts.notes: Just Security already published SPLC indictment analysis. Framework extends that coverage systematically. Pitch: Domain 28/29 as standalone analytical submission. Fastest credibility-building move available. Editorial submission path: justsecurity.org/submissions

contacts.name: Erica Chenoweth
contacts.organization: Harvard Kennedy School / Nonviolent Action Lab
contacts.role: Frank Stanton Professor of the First Amendment; Academic Dean for Faculty Development; Director, Nonviolent Action Lab
contacts.notes: 3.5% threshold is the single most-cited piece in the proposal. New book 'The End of People Power' (investigating decline of civil resistance success) makes the framework's resistance analysis directly relevant to her current research agenda. Pitch: methodological question about whether 3.5% threshold extrapolates from authoritarian to democratic-backsliding contexts.

contacts.name: Wendy R. Weiser
contacts.notes: Brennan Center review is itself a credibility signal. Controls the bridge node to Senate Judiciary and Rules Committee staff. Pitch: full proposal + specific domain brief on voting rights. Request feedback, not endorsement. Brennan Center already a primary source cited throughout the framework.

contacts.name: Ian Bassin
contacts.notes: Protect Democracy retaliatory action tracker is cited in Domain 29. Framework extends their documentation with structural analysis and reform proposals. Natural organizational partner. MacArthur Fellow 2023.

contacts.name: Marc Elias
contacts.notes: Democracy Docket tracks exactly the litigation the proposal addresses. His newsletter + Bluesky platform reach election lawyers and reform activists directly. High-profile media platform alongside legal work.

contacts.name: Sheldon Whitehouse
contacts.role: Senator (D-RI); Ranking Member, Judiciary Courts Subcommittee
contacts.contact: Contact via staff director, Senate Judiciary Democratic minority staff (202-224-5225)
contacts.notes: Lead sponsor, Supreme Court Ethics, Recusal and Transparency Act. Leads active SCOTUS ethics hearings. Domain 6 and Domain 29 map directly to his known legislative work. Contact staff director first — not the Senator. Send 1-page domain brief, not full document.

contacts.name: Dick Durbin
contacts.role: Senator (D-IL); Ranking Member, Senate Judiciary Committee
contacts.notes: Ranking Member, Judiciary — widest committee jurisdiction. Route through minority staff director. Frame as research resource for committee hearings, not a pitch. Best entry point: judiciary hearing prep materials.

contacts.name: Amy Klobuchar
contacts.role: Senator (D-MN); Ranking Member, Senate Rules and Administration Committee
contacts.notes: Former Chair, Senate Rules Committee. Lead author For the People Act. Election Mail Act, Election Worker Protection Act, Preparing Election Administrators for AI Act all introduced 2025-2026. Domain 1, 3, 37 are direct matches. Route through Rules Committee minority staff.

contacts.name: Jeff Merkley
contacts.notes: Blueprint for Democracy package: electoral college abolition, equal representation, filibuster reform. Joined Senate Rules Committee specifically to advance democracy reform. Running for reelection May 2026 — actively need policy research.

contacts.name: Elizabeth Warren
contacts.notes: Ban Presidential Plunder Act; ICE Accountability Act; SGE Ethics Enforcement and Reform Act. Anti-corruption framework aligns with Domains 5, 17, 20, 29. Staff director contact is the correct first step.

contacts.name: Ed Markey
contacts.notes: Lead sponsor, Supreme Court expansion bill. Climate-democracy nexus. Domain 6 and 15 are most relevant. Coordinate timing with Warren outreach — both MA senators, coordinate messaging.

contacts.name: Jamie Raskin
contacts.notes: Introduced legislation on Presidential Capacity Commission; Emoluments Clauses resolutions; Jan. 6 accountability. Most constitutionally engaged member of the House Judiciary minority. Route through minority staff director.

contacts.name: Sean Morales-Doyle
contacts.role: Director, Voting Rights and Elections Program
contacts.notes: Operational program director. More actionable than Waldman or Weiser for day-to-day domain-specific work. Media contact: Lexi Kennard kennardl@brennan.law.nyu.edu.

contacts.name: Michael Waldman
contacts.notes: Author of 'The Fight to Vote' and democracy framing aligns throughout. Send after Weiser/Morales-Doyle contact has been made — reference internal Brennan Center engagement.

contacts.name: Joanna Lydgate
contacts.notes: SUDC specializes in supporting state officials — direct connection to Domain 37 state-level election security gap. Most operationally relevant think tank for state AG coalition building.

contacts.name: Neera Tanden
contacts.notes: Full-spectrum progressive policy hub. Route through specific program director — domestic policy or national security — not the CEO directly. CAP has communications infrastructure for widespread policy brief distribution.

contacts.name: Heidi Shierholz
contacts.notes: Also serves on AFL-CIO board — dual bridge node to labor union distribution infrastructure. Pitch Domain 17 for EPI issue brief. EPI publication reaches every AFL-CIO union communications office.

contacts.name: Celine McNicholas
contacts.role: Director of Policy and General Counsel
contacts.notes: Primary operational contact at EPI for labor domain. NLRB dismantling analysis in Domain 17 directly relevant to her current work.

contacts.name: Josh Orton
contacts.notes: SCOTUS ethics, term limits, expansion is their explicit mission. Domain 6 analysis of term limits, ethics, expansion and their constitutional basis is directly usable for their campaigns.

contacts.name: Nicholas Stephanopoulos
contacts.role: Kirkland and Ellis Professor of Law; Director, Election Law Clinic
contacts.notes: Coined the efficiency gap metric — Domain 1 uses his framework for gerrymandering analysis. This is not a cold pitch: the framework is built substantially on his published work. Lead with the efficiency gap application and any extension or critique you have made of it.

contacts.name: Ruth Greenwood
contacts.role: Director, Voting Rights Litigation and Advocacy Clinic; Assistant Clinical Professor
contacts.contact: Contact via HLS Voting Rights Clinic page
contacts.notes: Clinical program director — decisions about what research to use in clinic litigation. Offer framework as litigation support resource. The litigation tracker is the most immediately actionable document for clinic work.

contacts.name: Larry Schwartztol
contacts.role: Professor of Practice; Faculty Director, Democracy and the Rule of Law Clinic
contacts.notes: Former White House voting rights and democracy reform lawyer. Now runs a clinic designed exactly for this type of research integration. Clinical adoption is the goal — offer to present to clinic students and supervising faculty.

contacts.name: Olatunde Johnson
contacts.organization: Columbia Law School / Constitutional Democracy Initiative
contacts.role: Ruth Bader Ginsburg '59 Professor of Law; Faculty Director, Constitutional Democracy Initiative
contacts.notes: CDI is the most structurally aligned law school center to this research. CDI holds seminars, publishes briefs, and convenes practitioners. Pitch as a research partnership and potential CDI seminar resource.

contacts.name: Pamela Karlan
contacts.role: Thomas Reid Professor of Law; Co-Director, Supreme Court Litigation Clinic
contacts.notes: Most prominent voting rights legal scholar in the country. Former DOJ Civil Rights Division AAG. SCOTUS Litigation Clinic means she trains Supreme Court practitioners. Framework's Domain 1 and Domain 6 are core to her work.

contacts.name: Erwin Chemerinsky
contacts.role: Dean; Jesse H. Choper Distinguished Professor of Law
contacts.notes: Co-launched 'We Hold These Truths' democracy education project. Law school dean who is actively running a parallel democratic education initiative — natural alignment. Pitch as complementary research resource for his existing project.

contacts.name: Anthony D. Romero
contacts.notes: Route through ACLU Voting Rights Project (voting@aclu.org) or Political Accountability Program for specific domain outreach. CEO-level contact is lower-probability; program director contact is more actionable.

contacts.name: Janai S. Nelson
contacts.notes: LDF active litigation docket overlaps with Domains 1, 14, 29. Offer litigation tracker as operational resource. VRA restoration analysis in Domain 1 and Domain 22 are primary entry points.

contacts.name: Damon T. Hewitt
contacts.organization: Lawyers' Committee for Civil Rights Under Law
contacts.notes: Lawyers' Committee co-leads Election Protection coalition. Direct path to the largest election-day legal protection operation in the country. Voting Rights Project (vrp@lawyerscommittee.org) is the operational team.

contacts.name: Virginia Kase Solomón
contacts.notes: Gerrymandering, campaign finance, voting rights are Common Cause's primary portfolio. Domains 1-3 map directly. Common Cause has state chapters in 35+ states — amplification beyond national HQ.

contacts.name: Phil Brest
contacts.notes: ACS has 200+ law school chapters and a Scholars Network connecting faculty across 200+ institutions. ACS Next Generation Leaders program moves people from law school to senior policy positions. ACS national convention 2026 is the key amplification event.

contacts.name: Rob Bonta
contacts.notes: California AG leads multi-state coalition litigation. Most resourced state AG office. Route through Deputy AG for Legislative Affairs or Constitutional Litigation division. Framework as analytical resource for coalition litigation strategy.

contacts.name: Dan Rayfield
contacts.notes: Has led or joined 52 lawsuits against the Trump administration — most active AG in the coalition. High receptivity to structured analytical frameworks for constitutional litigation.

contacts.name: Kris Mayes
contacts.notes: Arizona is a critical 2026 election state. Mayes is active in both immigration enforcement and election integrity litigation. Domain 37 is most urgent for her context.

contacts.name: Keith Ellison
contacts.notes: Prosecuted Derek Chauvin case. Labor and civil rights focus. Route through civil rights division. Criminal justice domain analysis (Domain 14) most relevant entry point.

contacts.name: Anthony Brown
contacts.notes: On the front lines of legal resistance to immigration, education, and healthcare cuts. Abrego Garcia case (Maryland) makes Domain 16 and Domain 29 directly relevant. Strong institutional alignment.

contacts.name: Shahrzad Shams
contacts.notes: Roosevelt's Democratic Institutions program is a direct structural match. Pipeline to progressive economic policy community. Todd Tucker (industrial policy) and Patrick Oakford (worker power) are adjacent Roosevelt contacts for Domains 17 and 23.

contacts.name: Leah Greenberg
contacts.notes: 1 million+ participants; 3,300 locations. Already uses Chenoweth's framework in training materials — direct entry point through the Chenoweth connection established in Batch 1. Warm intro path: through Erica Chenoweth after her engagement in Batch 1.

contacts.name: Heather Gerken
contacts.notes: Bridge node: controls Ford Foundation grantmaking ($1B+/year) AND has deep constitutional law expertise. A single program officer referral routes the framework to all relevant Ford grantees. Contact through Yale Law alumni network is the warm intro path.

contacts.name: Bryan Fair
contacts.notes: SPLC is the subject of the Domain 29 primary case study (April 21, 2026 indictment). Framework provides structural analysis useful for their legal defense narrative. Constitutional law professor — will engage with the legal analysis.

contacts.name: Danielle Allen
contacts.role: James Bryant Conant University Professor; Director, Allen Lab for Democracy Renovation
contacts.notes: Democracy Renovation Lab specifically focuses on institutional redesign for 21st-century governance — exact match with the framework's institutional design proposals. New book 'The Radical Duke' (2026, Liveright/Norton).

contacts.name: Steven Levitsky
contacts.role: David Rockefeller Professor of Government; Director, David Rockefeller Center for Latin American Studies
contacts.notes: 'How Democracies Die' (2018) and 'Tyranny of the Minority' (2023) are cited throughout the framework. 'The Price of Authoritarianism' (Foreign Affairs, Jan. 2025). Lead with the extension of his backsliding analysis to the 35-domain reform prescription — a gap he has noted exists in his own work.

contacts.name: Daniel Ziblatt
contacts.role: Eaton Professor of Government; Director, Center for European Studies
contacts.notes: Co-author with Levitsky. European comparative analysis. Contact after Levitsky — coordinate timing so they hear about the framework through peer channels.

contacts.name: Jack Balkin
contacts.role: Knight Professor of Constitutional Law and First Amendment
contacts.notes: Balkinization blog is read by every constitutional law professor in the country. A single post mentioning the framework reaches the entire top-50 law school faculty. Pitch: constitutional theory analysis in Domain 6 and Domain 8.

contacts.name: Archon Fung
contacts.role: Winthrop Laflin McCormack Professor; Director, Ash Center for Democratic Governance and Innovation
contacts.notes: Ash Center is the leading academic center on democratic innovation. Participatory budgeting and citizens' assembly designs in Domain 3 are Fung's primary research area. Ash Center publishes policy briefs — pitch the framework as a potential Ash publication.

contacts.name: Samuel Issacharoff
contacts.notes: Author of 'Fragile Democracies.' Election law, constitutional law, democratic theory — all three are primary framework domains. NYU's home of Brennan Center creates institutional synergy.

contacts.name: Quinta Jurecic
contacts.role: Staff Writer (The Atlantic); Fellow, Governance Studies (Brookings)
contacts.notes: War powers and prosecutorial weaponization are her established beats at The Atlantic. Formerly Lawfare senior editor. Pitch Domains 28 and 29 as analytical frameworks for her ongoing coverage.

contacts.name: Zack Beauchamp
contacts.notes: Global democracy beat is a direct match for the framework's comparative analysis. Pitch: '35 things structurally broken in American democracy and what 40 other countries did about them' — Vox explainer format is perfectly aligned.

contacts.name: Benjamin Wittes
contacts.notes: Lawfare's readership is the legal-security policy community — federal courts coverage, Senate staff, national security attorneys. After Just Security publication (Goodman), pitch Lawfare as secondary legal platform. Less likely to be receptive to first-contact unsolicited pitches.

contacts.name: Heather Cox Richardson
contacts.organization: Letters from an American (Substack) / Boston College
contacts.notes: 2.9 million subscribers — single largest democracy-oriented newsletter in the country. Do NOT contact before at least one Tier 1 institutional citation exists. Correct path: get cited by Levitsky/Ziblatt or Chenoweth first, then Richardson encounters it organically. If direct email: clean, sourced, factual — not a pitch.

contacts.name: Paul Glastris
contacts.notes: Washington Monthly routinely publishes extended structural reform analysis. Glastris has published previous reform frameworks — the 35-domain document is the right format for his magazine. Pitch the executive summary with offer to provide the full framework.

contacts.name: Ryan Grim
contacts.notes: Drop Site News has covered the SPLC indictment and Venezuela operation. May be interested in the structural analysis behind current investigative stories.

contacts.name: William Baude
contacts.notes: Published the leading Section 3 disqualification analysis. Domain 37's Section 3 certification recovery strategy builds on his published framework. Direct citation — lead with this connection.

contacts.name: Kenji Yoshino
contacts.role: Chief Justice Earl Warren Professor; Faculty Director, Meltzer Center
contacts.notes: Meltzer Center for Diversity, Inclusion, and Belonging. Constitutional law and civil rights framing. Domain 7 rights protection analysis is the primary entry point.

contacts.name: Theda Skocpol
contacts.role: Victor S. Thomas Professor of Government and Sociology
contacts.notes: 'Diminished Democracy' is foundational to the organizational depth argument in the framework. Her policy feedback theory underlies the civic infrastructure analysis. Lead with the specific citation and extension.

contacts.name: Jacob Hacker
contacts.role: Stanley B. Resor Professor of Political Science
contacts.notes: 'Winner-Take-All Politics' and 'Power and Progress' framing aligns with the economic concentration and labor sections. Academic credibility in political economy space.

contacts.name: Molly Reynolds
contacts.role: Vice President and Director, Governance Studies
contacts.notes: Brookings Governance Studies publishes policy briefs read by Hill staff. Route through governance@brookings.edu — identify the specific program analyst whose portfolio matches the domain.

contacts.name: Eliza Sweren-Becker
contacts.role: Deputy Director, Voting Rights and Elections Program
contacts.notes: Operational deputy to Sean Morales-Doyle. More accessible for working-level collaboration than program director. Election law practitioner — offer the litigation tracker as a working resource.

contacts.name: Suzanne Mettler
contacts.role: John L. Senior Professor of American Institutions
contacts.notes: 'The Submerged State' is foundational to the social safety net visibility analysis in Domain 18. Civic capacity research connects to Domain 3. Citation and extension approach.

contacts.name: Abbe Gluck
contacts.role: Professor of Law; Co-Director, Solomon Center for Health Law and Policy
contacts.notes: Healthcare law expert at Yale — Solomon Center is the natural home for healthcare-democracy nexus research. Domain 31 (OBBBA Medicaid) is the most timely entry point. Pending health domain expansion makes this contact more valuable in Phase 2.

contacts.name: Jamelle Bouie
contacts.notes: Race, democracy, American political history. Historical lens on Domains 1, 14, 15, 22. NYT column has significant reach into the policy-engaged readership. Pitch the historical case studies section.

contacts.name: Liz Shuler
contacts.notes: Also AFL-CIO board chair. Dual bridge node: AFL-CIO (12.5M members) + EPI (policy research arm). Do not contact before EPI issue brief is published. AFL-CIO distribution follows EPI publication automatically.

contacts.name: April Verrett
contacts.notes: SEIU re-affiliated with AFL-CIO January 2025. Represents healthcare workers directly affected by OBBBA Medicaid crisis (Domain 31). First Black SEIU president. Domain 17 sectoral bargaining model is most relevant.

contacts.name: Claude Cummings Jr.
contacts.notes: CWA represents media workers, tech workers, and telecom workers. Domain 17 sectoral bargaining model matches CWA's organizing structure most closely. Pitch Domain 17 alongside Domain 8 (media) given CWA's unique cross-sector membership.

contacts.name: Shawn Fain
contacts.notes: Domain 23 (tariff unilateralism) is directly relevant to auto industry. EV transition just-transition analysis (Domain 15) connects to UAW's contract concerns. Trade policy framing is the entry point.

contacts.name: Derrick Johnson
contacts.notes: NAACP's 2,200 branches make this a major amplification node. Route through national policy office before CEO. After NAACP LDF engagement (T1-027), this is the mass membership follow-on.

contacts.name: Rashad Robinson
contacts.notes: Color of Change runs digital advocacy campaigns with mass reach. Platform accountability work (Domain 8) is a distinctive overlap. Criminal justice domain analysis directly supports their district attorney accountability campaigns.

contacts.name: Janet Murguía
contacts.notes: 300+ member organizations. Immigration and economic justice focus. Domain 16 (immigration courts, community alternatives to detention, pathway to legal status) is the primary entry point.

contacts.name: Ana Maria Archila
contacts.notes: Grassroots organizing at state and local level. Bridge to tenant rights, immigration, and labor coalitions simultaneously.

contacts.name: Amara Enyia
contacts.notes: M4BL's BREATHE Act frames the fiscal case similarly to Domain 22. Direct policy team contact is more actionable than leadership. After Chenoweth engagement provides movement research legitimacy.

contacts.name: Dianna Wynn
contacts.notes: League has 800+ chapters nationwide. Voter registration and election administration are their core operations. Domain 1's automatic voter registration and RCV provisions map directly. Celina Stewart (CEO) is the operational contact.

contacts.name: Leslie Graves
contacts.notes: 49 jurisdictions now using RCV, 14 million voters. FairVote is the primary national advocate for Domain 1's ranked-choice proposals. Direct implementation partner — offer framework as supporting research for their advocacy.

contacts.name: Nicole Ozer
contacts.notes: EFF's FOIA suit against CMS for Medicaid AI decision records is directly relevant to Domain 36 (AI governance). Surveillance, digital rights, First Amendment online — all are core EFF portfolio items matching framework domains.

contacts.name: Alexandra Reeve Givens
contacts.notes: CDT covers AI governance, election security, privacy, and platform accountability — four framework domains. AI governance overlap is the highest-value entry point given current AI policymaking urgency.

contacts.name: Rev. William Barber II
contacts.notes: Poor People's Campaign operates at the intersection of faith and policy advocacy. Moral framework for democracy reform aligns with the research's normative arguments. Reach: 200+ coalition organizations across 45 states.

contacts.name: Adam Russell Taylor
contacts.notes: Progressive Christian political engagement. Sojourners has a publication with a policy-engaged faith readership. The moral framing of the fiscal reform proposals is the entry point.

contacts.name: Meredith Whittaker
contacts.contact: Contact via AI Now Institute, NYU
contacts.notes: AI Now Institute at NYU is the leading academic institution on AI governance and civil rights. Large public platform. Domain 36 (AI Governance and Algorithmic Democracy) is the direct match.

contacts.name: Albert Fox Cahn
contacts.notes: S.T.O.P. tracks LPR surveillance, biometrics, and government surveillance law specifically. Flock Safety LPR contract changes documented in surveillance-tracking.md are relevant to their current campaigns.

contacts.name: A.J. D'Amico
contacts.notes: Knight's Information and Society program is the most aligned foundation program to the framework's media and digital domains. A program director referral routes the framework to relevant Knight grantees.

contacts.name: Open Society Fellowship Program
contacts.notes: OSF Fellowship is designed exactly for independent research projects of this type. The 35-domain proposal qualifies on its face. Submit independently of institutional contacts — this is not a referral program.

contacts.name: John C. Yang
contacts.notes: AAJC tracks civil rights enforcement for AAPI communities — immigration, civil liberties, voting rights. Student visa revocations (Domain 27) are an active issue in this community.

contacts.name: Raúl Torrez
contacts.notes: Active in multi-state coalition litigation. New Mexico is a critical border state for immigration and environmental enforcement issues.

contacts.name: Marc Morial
contacts.notes: National Urban League has 100 affiliates in 36 states. Economic empowerment and civil rights focus. Housing (Domain 13) and labor (Domain 17) are most concrete entry points.
